---
title: 'The Day of Christ’s Return Part 2'
date: '2012-01-21T09:45:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '8410278136068042897'
blogger_filename: '/2012/01/the-day-of-christs-return-part-2.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Studies'
  - 'Prophecy'
---

<br /><h1 style="background-color: white; border-bottom-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-bottom-style: dotted; border-bottom-width: 1px; color: #333333; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 20px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 10px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 5px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-decoration: none;"> The Day of Christ’s Return Part 2 – The&nbsp;Moon</h1><h2 style="background-color: white; border-bottom-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-bottom-style: dotted; border-bottom-width: 1px; color: #333333; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 20px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 10px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 5px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-decoration: none;"> The Day and Hour No Man Knows… The Moon</h2><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><em><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+24:36&amp;version=KJV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Matthew 24:36</a></strong></em><br /><em>But of that day and hour&nbsp;<strong>know</strong>eth&nbsp;<strong>no</strong>&nbsp;<strong>man</strong>,&nbsp;<strong>no</strong>,&nbsp;<strong>no</strong>t the angels of heaven, but my Father only.</em></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><em><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+25:13&amp;version=KJV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Matthew 25:13</a></strong></em><br /><em>Watch therefore, for ye&nbsp;<strong>know</strong>&nbsp;neither the day&nbsp;<strong>no</strong>r the hour wherein the Son of&nbsp;<strong>man</strong>&nbsp;cometh.</em></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><em><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+13:32&amp;version=KJV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Mark 13:32</a></strong></em><br /><em>But of that day and that hour&nbsp;<strong>know</strong>eth&nbsp;<strong>no</strong>&nbsp;<strong>man</strong>,&nbsp;<strong>no</strong>,&nbsp;<strong>no</strong>t the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.</em></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">If God says something once it’s important. If he says it more than once we need to pay careful attention to it, meditate on it, and make sure we understand what He is saying. In order to properly understand anything in scripture we MUST begin to understand Hebrew, Greek, and Ancient culture. We cannot take idioms written to an eastern audience and assume we understand them in a western mindset. For those who are bilingual this should be an easy thing to understand. Just translating Spanish to English can be hard sometimes and we share the similar cultures and economies. We use many of the same words. But a saying such as: “He’s a shoe in” meaning that he’s is sure to win, does not necessarily translate to Spanish. They may hear that he is a shoe and look at you VERY cock eyed?</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Here we have such an issue. Even if we lay aside Idioms, the only thing we know for certain by evaluating this scripture in English is that know one in Jesus’ Audience including himself knew the day or hour. He said, present tense, “no man knows” not that no man would ever know. Even at face value it’s not as clear as people draw it to be. However let’s take a deeper look at what he said.</div><h3 style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 18px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 5px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-decoration: none;"> <strong>The New Moon</strong></h3><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">But let us take a look at just such an idiom. The New Moon Festival. The new moon was the start of each month and year on the Jewish Calendar. See the Following:</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.jewfaq.org/calendar.htm" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Judaism 101: Jewish Calendar</a></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">The lunar month on the Jewish calendar begins when the first sliver of moon becomes visible after the dark of the moon. In ancient times, the new months used to be determined by observation. When people observed the new moon, they would notify the Sanhedrin. When the Sanhedrin heard testimony from two independent, reliable eyewitnesses that the new moon occurred on a certain date, they would declare the&nbsp;<a href="http://www.jewfaq.org/defs/chodesh.htm" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">rosh chodesh</a>&nbsp;(first of the month) and send out messengers to tell people when the month began.</div><dl style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px;"><dt><strong><a href="http://www.jewfaq.org/chodesh.htm" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Rosh Chodesh</a>&nbsp;(ROHSH CHOH-desh)</strong></dt><dd style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 5px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Lit. head of the month. The first day of a month, on which the first sliver of the new moon appears. It is a minor festival today, though it was a more significant festival in ancient times.</dd></dl><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">The date of the new month and the new year was to be set by those placed in charge of the Temple. They would use ancient methods to watch for signs and calculate the New Moon. This would allow them to declare the new month or year. Because this date was impossible to know until JUST before it happened it became known as the<strong>&nbsp;“Day and Hour no man knows.”</strong></div><h3 style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 18px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 5px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-decoration: none;"> <strong>The Church is The Moon</strong></h3><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Jesus is referred to as the Sun and we are the light to this darkened world. It stands to reason then that if He is the Sun we are the Moon. All things given under the Old Covenant are shadows of New Covenant Realities. If they celebrate every new moon so should we, knowing that we are that new moon.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Malachi+4:2&amp;version=KJV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Malachi 4:2</a></strong><br />But unto you that fear my name shall the&nbsp;<strong>Sun</strong>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>righteousness</strong>&nbsp;arise with healing in his wings; and ye shall go forth, and grow up as calves of the stall.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1%20Corinthians+15:41&amp;version=KJV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">1 Corinthians 15:41</a></strong><br />There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the&nbsp;<strong>moon</strong>, and another glory of the stars: for one star differeth from another star in glory.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+5:14&amp;version=KJV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Matthew 5:14</a></strong><br /><strong>Ye</strong>&nbsp;are the&nbsp;<strong>light</strong>&nbsp;of the&nbsp;<strong>world</strong>. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.</div><h3 style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 18px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 5px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-decoration: none;"> <strong>Closing Thoughts</strong></h3><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">We are the New Moon, or we will be when He comes. His Coming is about bringing us New Life, New Bodies, New everything. His coming will become clearer each day that it becomes closer. Those who are trained and ready and watching will be able to announce the day just as they did in Ancient Israel. But trusted people, with a track record of being spot on will have to be those people. Not some kooks who have a track record of making wild predictions that turn out to be wrong.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">The day is coming… I’m watching… are you?</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+24:32&amp;version=KJV" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">Matthew 24:32</a></strong><br />Now learn a parable of the&nbsp;<strong>fig</strong>&nbsp;<strong>tree</strong>; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh:</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Israel is our time piece, you want to know what’s going on on God’s prophetic calendar keep an eye on them!</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Darrell G. Wolfe</div>
